body_txt: When in a battle to the death, the winner has ultimate authority over the loser's fate. Often they will just kill them, but why take part of their corpse , if you can take them alive and torment them for the rest of their days. Maybe the villain will bind the hero, and if they are politically powerful, they will probably parade their prize in front of those who have Come to Gawk . The fun part for the villain is definitely in the Trophy Room . Confinement situations can vary; they may be hung up in cages like canaries , kept in a glass case with a label , dressed up in a bikini to dance provocatively , suspended in inescapable energy , classic Bound and Gagged , kept in a block of ice or even turned to stone. No matter how they are kept, they will be out in the open or up on a pedestal, and will be hard to miss on the Christmas card. They may be unconscious or forever awake. Keeping them like this is not out of monetary necessity, especially if they have to be fed, but usually immobilized prisoners do not have to be fed or cleaned in fiction. It is all purely Sadistic , as physical proof that they have defeated the foe, stringing the loser up and making them look pretty like a shiny medal or blue ribbon. Of course the physical torment is enjoyed by the villain if they keep the foe conscious, but the main appeal for the villain is the daily agonizing humiliation the once great warrior faces, and the strength of the villain to keep them trapped for the rest of their life. If this captivity or bondage is mystical in origin, then the only means of liberating oneself from such a deal without serious cosmic repercussions is when the master is dead or finally defeated . A particularly horrifying or extreme example of being Defeated and Trophied might fall under And I Must Scream or a Fate Worse than Death . Compare Battle Trophy , Creepy Souvenir , and Dead Guy on Display , which all require the trophy to be dead. See also Deal with the Devil , Human Pet , Living Doll Collector , Living Museum Exhibit , and People Zoo . Not to be confused with Lost Him in a Card Game , Trophy Child , or Trophy Wife . Can result in Why Don't You Just Shoot Him?
